channel.title: Recruitment (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Recruitment" - For PF to succeed, we're going to need a whole bunch of people with a wide variety of skill sets. If you want to contribute, please sign up in the appropriate recruitment thread(s).

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Username: Hollows

Experience: I am an IT professional with 4 years of experience in system administration, technical support, and information management. Experience maintaining 24/7/365 and/or 100% availability systems for local and state governments, in addition to creating custom solutions for customers using a variety of technologies depending on the customer's needs.

Professional Experience: I have experience in a number of IT roles, almost all with government systems. One of my previous roles consisted of primarily tier one IT support, including customer support, router ans switch maintenance, 0365 and Exchange maintenance, server administration, AP installation, VLAN configuration, etc. Another role was in IT project management, converting outdated organization frameworks to modern ITIL, ServiceNow, and Jira frameworks where applicable.

My most recent role included all aforementioned tier one duties with the addition of some IT management, including creating additions to the business continuity plan and disaster recovery plan. In this role, I also managed certain parts of FOIA requests, maintaining content that should be available to the public 100% of the time to comply with government oversight and transparency law.

Technical documentation is a specialty of mine. I have been hired before based solely on the quality of my documentation and technical writing.

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: bachelor's degree in Information Technology w/ magna cum laude honors from my program. Was president of local association of IT professionals for two years, expert with networking and coordination.

What technologies are you familiar with in your area?: I am familiar with a myriad of resource management and issue tracking systems, such as ServiceNow, Jira, KACE, and custom tracking solutions based on O365 SharePoint and WordPress. Experience with Docker, PowerShell, Bash, Kubernetes, and VMWare for the fully automated creation, configuration, and implementation of containerized VMs with 0 downtime. Creation and maintenance of containerized full-stack Elasticsearch-Logstash-Kibana data collection and visualization system.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design: Re: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design: Re: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design
item.content:encoded:
Username: Dr Arkelao
Drawings: Currently I don't have online portfolio.
Skills: Vector graphics in Illustrator, typography in Glyps, layout in InDesign, drawing in Photoshop and Procreate and animation in After Effects and Toon Boom Harmony. Also I dont if it fits here but I’m learning pixel art with Aseprite.
Applying for: Whatever is needed.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design: Re: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design

Experience —> I've had my paintings sold in art shows but I'm not a professional artist, I draw for fun and I'd love to create some illustrations for the site :)

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design: Re: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design
item.content:encoded:
Not sure it applies, but I joined to contribute so might as well offer my services here, in case they might be needed.

I enjoy creating technical drawings, schematics and sketches.

3D modelling (Technical) : Autocad, Solidworks.
3D modelling (Creative) : Z-Brush, Sculptris, Sketchup.
Circuit design: (Functional and creative) : Eagle, Ulti, Designspark
2D design / Vector design: Inkscape, GIMP, Corel.

Physical skills: Graffiti and stencil art to do so (in some cases).

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
• Username: Proteus Steinmetz
  • Applying For: System Administration
  • Experience: If this site truly requires a proper system administrator, then I can assist the SCP Wiki team in this field. I have about 8 years of experience in system administration, and I am currently a full-time penetration tester and cybersecurity researcher. As a system administrator, I have experience across various operating systems (Solaris, Red Hat Linux, Windows Server, MacOS, etc.) and software management suites. I also have experience with servers, IT security systems, and other critical components of an organization's IT infrastructure. I also have a strong background in system automation and virtualization.
  • Professional Experience: I have experience managing critical infrastructure for the Department of Defense, the Federal Aviation Administration, and the United States Air Force. I was in charge of the primary and secondary networks that handled real-time air traffic control operations for the FAA at multiple sites. In the Air Force, I was the lead member of the radar network responsible for providing weather and air traffic data to both military and civilian satellite sites. For the Department of Defense, I managed thousands of classified machines, intrusion detection systems, and user accounts as well as sensitive proprietary applications.
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skillset: Bachelor's in Information Technology with a security focus, CompTIA Linux+, CompTIA Network+, CompTIA Security+, OSCP, OSCE, Certified Ethical Hacker v10, Penetration Testing Professional v5, Certified Information Systems Security Professional, Certified Information Systems Auditor, Coined and awarded an Air Force achievement medal by Chief of Staff General Mark A. Welsh III of the United States Air Force for identifying and designing a fix action for a significant network design flaw, AFSC Oustanding Airman of the Year across the global Air Force talent pool, Awarded SrA Below-The-Zone, Diamond Sharp Award, Air Force Air Mobility Command Inspector General's Outstanding Performer Award
  • What technologies are you familiar with within your area of expertise? For instance, do you use Jira to track QA issues? Do you work with JUnit for Unit tests? Ever used Docker? Tell me about your abilities here: JIRA is fine enough, the government uses its own proprietary system, but the only issue with JIRA is if you have someone set it up poorly. The program also handles emails pretty wonky. My last thing to note about it is that it gets expensive quickly as the user pool increases for licenses. For unit testing, I enjoy TestNG over JUnit since it has a bit more functions available but JUnit is fine too. I prefer to use Kubernetes in general, but it also works well alongside Docker. I have a lot of automation experience since in sysadmin that's the name of the game. I love bash and batch scripting but a few languages I have direct experience with are SQL, C, C++, Perl, Python, C#, Java, Powershell, and .NET as a technology in general. On the cybersecurity side of the house, I employ Kali Linux as most do, with BurpSuite and Metasploit.

Overall, I've pretty much used most of the standard sysadmin tools over the years and can learn anything new quickly based on prior experience.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills: Re: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills
item.content:encoded:
Username: Carrington

Experience: I began programming when I was 5 and my AIS PhD mother hit me over the head with a book on C and said do this.

Professional Experience: I began consulting as a college gig in 2004 and I became a full time developer in 2008. I am a web developer by primary inclination. I can't post my full CV for personal reasons, but I can say I have worked for a Fortune 500 enterprise as well as several startups, one unicorn. My titles have been formally FE for the last couple of years, but I am a fullstack engineer by nature. I'm happy to share specifics (or the full CV if you'd like) over PM.

Professional Accolades and Et Cetera: I've received spot bonuses and assorted internal awards at various firms for mentorship and modernization.

Language Proficiencies:

  • Javascript, CSS, HTML of course. Node included. I've used most major frameworks professionally, but I have the most experience with React and Vue… and Angular 1, but please no?
  • I've been known to help normalize a relational database or two
  • C# (for Unity, but also for love)
  • There was a time I did PHP for 5 years but I've been clean for a while
  • Ruby if you're into that
  • Enough C/Scheme/C++ for WASM and to get myself otherwise in trouble
  • I have a curious attraction to neo4j that I can't shake

I will also have to learn Go at some point because my current firm uses it on the BE but I would not call myself 'proficient' by any stretch.

More seriously, this site means a huge amount to me. I really can't overstate how much. I would love to help however I can.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills: Re: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills
item.content:encoded:
Username: Dr Shiba Nasu
Experience: I have been programming as a hobby for about 4 years now thought i am still more near the beginner level in some programming language's but experienced in others
Professional Experience: I have never done any work around my programming so i don't have anything to put down here
Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications: I don't have any seeing as i have programmed as a hobby and learned everything i know from websites and online copys of textbooks
Language proficiency I have put most my time into python but i have also put a lot of time into Html CSS JavaScript. i also know little bits of C++ Ruby and java

I am pretty sure i wont get the job infact you guys probaly not recruiting anymore but i want to help with anything i can.

Language proficiencies: Python, html, shell and derivates
Note: I might not be very active, due to having long days and lots of homeworks, but I'll do my best.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills: Re: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills
item.content:encoded:
Username: UraniumEmpire

Experience: Bachelors in computer science with an emphasis on development and data management. Notable projects include a game bot and a light-seeking solar panel.

Professional Experience: N/A

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications: Looking into security certification, otherwise N/A

Language Proficiencies:

  • Programming Languages: Java, C#, Python
  • Operating Systems: Windows XP/7/8/10, CentOS, Ubuntu
  • Web Development Languages: HTML, CSS, Javascript
  • Web Frameworks: ASP.net
  • Database Environments: MySQL, PostgreSQL

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Username: ManyMeats

Experience: I am an IT professional with 12 years of experience in systems administration and technical support. Previous jobs include 'Solutions Engineer' for a managed services firm, 'Network Engineer' for a Fortune 500 company in their network operations center, a 'Storage and Backup Engineer' for a US leader in insurance, and most recently full system support for a State agency in Wisconsin. During this time, I have accumulated a tremendous level of exposure to different technologies including more than half of my professional career building, supporting, and maintaining 24/7/365 systems in a data center or hosted environment.

Professional Experience: I would list myself as "advanced" in the following items: Windows Server 2008-2016, Office 365, Citrix VDI-in-a-box, VMWare (especially NTX and Horizons), Commvault backup and recovery, Hitachi and NetApp SANS, SQL Server, Powershell, and Cisco switches.

I would consider myself "intermediate" or to have tertiary experience with the following items: RedHat (5, 6, 7), Oracle (11, 12), Veeam, Adaptive firewall appliances such as Cisco ASAs Palo Alto devices, racking and cabling, cable termination, and other general data center tasks. I'm sure there are other miscellaneous things I could probably list.

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: I have a Bachelors in Management Information Systems and a Bachelors in Business Administration (w/ minor in CompSci). I have the following vendor-specific accreditations: Commvault Certified Engineer, VMware Certified Professional, CCNA-Routing&Switching, A+ Certification.

What technologies are you familiar with in your area?: I am familiar with a number of issue and ticket tracking items such as Spiceworks, ServiceNow, and Numera Footprints. In the IT world, fast adaptation to things like this is necessary for survival. Additionally, I do a lot of cross-work with application developers, so I am familiar with github and Jira for iteration and issue tracking, however I am not a frequent end-user for these softwares myself.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if your still accepting people but i feel i could help with finding bigs and errors n stuff.

Username: Vulcov
Experience: i like breaking things and finding where they don't work. I've done it before for video/computer games for a couple friends in the past and helped test some website features a couple years back for a buddy.
Technologies: well… i have a baseball bat… no but seriously I have a computer and an obsessive compulsion to learn just tell me what you'll need point me in the right direction and i'll see what I can do.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Management: Re: Recruitment Thread - Management
item.content:encoded:
Username: Erikole211

Experience:
2016 6 Month Internship at Zwilling (a german knive manufactorer) for my university
in the personnel administration.
2017 3 Month Internship, again at Zwilling, in the marketing administration.

Certifications: I made my economic psychology Bachelor in 2016 and i am currently working on
my Master.

I dont know how much work this will be, so i have to add that i am (as said above) currently working on my master, so i wont be able to spend 8hours a day.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills: Re: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills
item.content:encoded:
Username: AimEngie

Experience: Well my education is primarily in robotics engineering. I build and program midsize industrial robots. Aside from that I regularly work with kernel mods for linux.

Professional Experience: None aside from consulting on an app that I don't even know came out yet, and building/programming a small robot for a scholarship.

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications: Some computer science credits via Siena, some engineering credits from RIT, nothing huge.

Language proficiencies: Java, Python, HTML
